title: Creating Tensor Maps
author: Courtney Comrie
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8jyhupw

steps.title: This protocol will present the steps...
steps.instruction: This protocol will present the steps needed to compute multiple tensor maps on the HPC in TORTOISE. The protocol assumes that DIFFPREP and DRBUDDI have already been applied.

steps.title: Open up ITK-snap to create mask
steps.instruction: Open up ITK-snap to create mask. Type the following in a terminal: cd /rsgrps/hutchinsone/Singularity_Containersmodule load singularitysingularity run nklab-neurotools-v0.4.sif itksnap

steps.title: Once ITK-SNAP opens browse to the structural image
steps.instruction: Once ITK-SNAP opens browse to the structural image. The folder you should be pulling the structural.nii image from is in DTITest_DTI_proc_DRBUDDI_proc

steps.title: Click on the snake tool to start making the mask
steps.instruction: Click on the snake tool to start making the mask.

steps.title: Move the red lines to adjust...
steps.instruction: Move the red lines to adjust boundaries for the fill function.

steps.title: Select Segment 3D after you finish adjusting the red lines
steps.instruction: Select Segment 3D after you finish adjusting the red lines.

steps.title: Select the blue thresholding view and...
steps.instruction: Select the blue thresholding view and then adjust your thresholding till you are satisfied.

steps.title: For thresholding typically you want to...
steps.instruction: For thresholding typically you want to remove bone, muscle tissue, and eyes if possible.

steps.title: Click the Add Bubble at Cursor...
steps.instruction: Click the Add Bubble at Cursor button while clicking around on all the 3 different views to move the cursor.

steps.title: Select Next in the lower right hand corner
steps.instruction: Select Next in the lower right hand corner.

steps.title: Select the play button to grow the bubbles
steps.instruction: Select the play button to grow the bubbles. You can additionally select the update button at the bottom of the screen to view your growing mask.

steps.title: Once you have added enough "bubbles" select next
steps.instruction: Once you have added enough "bubbles" select next.

steps.title: When the mask is done select...
steps.instruction: When the mask is done select the pause button and click finish.

steps.title: You can select update a second...
steps.instruction: You can select update a second time and your completed mask will appear in the lower left hand corner.

steps.title: Save your mask as a NIFTI...
steps.instruction: Save your mask as a NIFTI file, and exit out of ITK-SNAP.

steps.title: Type the following to get information about the command
steps.instruction: Type the following to get information about the command. EstimateTensorNLLS

steps.title: Type the following
steps.instruction: Type the following: EstimateTensorNLSS -i directory_DRBUDDI_proc/file_name.list -m directory/mask_name.nii

steps.title: This creates two files a _DT
steps.instruction: This creates two files a _DT.nii and a _AM.nii. You will use the _DT.nii file.

steps.title: To create every tensor map type the following command
steps.instruction: To create every tensor map type the following command: ComputeAllTensorMaps.bash directory/tensor_file_name.nii

steps.title: The above command runs a script...
steps.instruction: The above command runs a script to create all tensor maps available, but if you want to only create a certain tensor map you can use can just type: ComputeDECMap -i directory/tensor_file_name.nii This example will only output a DEC map, but if you want to output another map just change the 'DEC' to be the name of the desired map.

steps.title: Open your DEC map in ITK-SNAP
steps.instruction: Open your DEC map in ITK-SNAP.

steps.title: Click on the Layer Inspector button:
steps.instruction: Click on the Layer Inspector button:

steps.title: Choose your display mode to be RGB Display
steps.instruction: Choose your display mode to be RGB Display.

steps.title: Below is the finished RGB display of your DEC map
steps.instruction: Below is the finished RGB display of your DEC map.
